claim.observation: Parsing Security.evtx (icat inode 103887 -> /tmp/sec.evtx) and extracting all 4697 'service installed' records (event identifier 0x00001259) yields 12 events, all on computer base-dc.shieldbase.lan, dated Sep 06 2018. Two are non-standard: a service named 'F-Response Subject' with image 'C:\windows\subject_srv.exe -s "base-hunt.shieldbase.lan:5682" -l 3262 -v "F-Response Subject" -k "155522845"', and a service named 'mnemosyne' with image 'C:\windows/Mnemosyne.sys' (this driver service installed three times). The remaining ten are routine Windows Defender driver services (MpKsl657a2bb2, MpKsl68a89100) and per-user svchost services ('svchost.exe -k UnistackSvcGroup').
claim.interpretation: Two of the installed services on this DC are not native OS components: (a) 'F-Response Subject' / subject_srv.exe is consistent with a commercial remote disk-and-memory forensic-acquisition agent, configured by its command line to connect to host base-hunt.shieldbase.lan on TCP 5682; (b) 'mnemosyne' is a kernel-mode driver (.sys) loaded from C:\windows, recorded as installed three times. The remaining ten are routine Windows Defender driver services (MpKsl*) and per-user svchost services. These Security-log 4697 records indicate that service installations for a remote-acquisition-style agent and a kernel driver were logged on this DC on Sep 06 2018. This may be material context for the shadow-copy/collection commands in finding-004 and the staged C:\temp copies in finding-001; the log alone does not indicate the authorization or purpose of those activities.

evidence_pointer.locator: inode 103887; 4697 records at Sep 06 2018 (e.g. 08:30:02 UTC); host base-dc.shieldbase.lan
